notes_on_cap: Lions were over the cap pre-FA. Restructured Jared Goff's contract, converting ~$40M of base salary into a signing bonus, creating approximately $32M in new cap space. Combined with releases of Glasgow and Decker (saving ~$17M+), team moved from negative effective cap space to approximately $35M available per OverTheCap post-FA.

key_departures.impact: 10-year Lions starter at LT requested his release after discussions with the team. Saved $11.6M in cap space. Created a glaring hole at left tackle — the biggest positional departure of the offseason. Penei Sewell may shift to LT.

Released ahead of free agency, saving $5.5M. Glasgow had struggled at center in 2025 after sliding over from guard following Frank Ragnow's retirement. Departure escalated the IOL/center need the Lions already had.

Traded to the Texans for IOL Juice Scruggs, a 2026 4th-round pick, and a 2027 7th-round pick. Saved ~$3.5M on the cap. Left a hole as Jahmyr Gibbs' primary complement. Creates a need for a bruiser/RB2.

Had a career-best 11 sacks in 2025 playing opposite Aidan Hutchinson. Signed a 1-year deal worth up to $6M with Tampa. His departure, along with Tyrus Wheat and Josh Paschal, leaves Detroit dangerously thin at EDGE behind Hutchinson.

Multi-year defensive captain signed a 2-year, $17M deal with Tampa Bay. Departure removes the veteran signal-caller from Detroit's LB room. Jack Campbell (All-Pro) and Derrick Barnes return, but depth is now thin.

Signed a 2-year, $16M deal with Washington. Was filling a near-starter role due to Detroit's injury woes in 2025. Departure leaves CB depth thin, especially with Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) recovering.
Signed a 2-year, $10.5M-$11.5M deal with Arizona. Had 2 sacks in his lone Detroit season. Departure, combined with DT DJ Reader also departing, strips the Lions' interior defensive line depth.
Veteran nose tackle departing via UFA. Combined with Lopez's departure, the Lions' interior D-line is significantly thinner heading into the draft.

Signed a 1-year, $1.755M deal with Dallas. Was not tendered by the Lions. Primarily a special teams contributor (88 defensive snaps, 86 special teams snaps in 2025). Loss is manageable on defense but hurts ST.
Released, saving $1.66M. Missed entire 2025 season on IR. Depth loss on paper but had not contributed meaningfully due to injury.
Signed a 2-year, $4.1M (up to $6.1M) deal with Buffalo. Created a QB2 vacancy that the Lions immediately addressed with Teddy Bridgewater.
Signed a 1-year, $5.1M (reported as $3.5M by some sources) deal with Chicago. Five-year Lion departs. Primarily a slot/return specialist. Hurts ST depth and slot receiver depth.
Signed a 2-year deal worth up to $5.05M with the Rams. Led team with 375 special teams snaps in 2025. Significant special teams loss on top of the Anzalone departure.

signings.analysis: Detroit's top priority entering FA was center, created by Frank Ragnow's retirement and Graham Glasgow's release/struggles. Mays, 26, was a starter for Carolina in 2025, allowing zero sacks on 726 snaps with a 94.3% pass block win rate (20th among qualified centers). The 3-year deal with $14M guaranteed is a real commitment — this is their center. The $8.3M AAV is mid-starter tier, reasonable for the market. IOL drops from a critical tier-1 need to a depth/developmental consideration. Juice Scruggs (acquired via Montgomery trade) also adds depth/competition at center/guard.

contract.notes: Existing contract, $0 guaranteed remaining. Acquired as part of Montgomery trade along with 2026 4th-round pick and 2027 7th-round pick.

signings.analysis: Former 2nd-round pick (2023) with 1,663 career snaps at both guard spots and center. No guaranteed money remaining on his contract — purely a low-risk evaluation. Profiles as a capable backup at center and below-average spot starter at guard. Adds IOL depth but is not a starter solution on his own. The real value here was the 2026 4th-round pick acquired alongside him.

contract.notes: 1-year deal; exact guarantees not reported. Some sources cite ~$2.5M per SI, others have terms TBD. Using SI/reported figure.

signings.analysis: Detroit native signed a 1-year deal after Taylor Decker's release created an urgent LT vacancy. Borom is penciled in as the starting RT with Penei Sewell potentially shifting to LT, though the Lions are expected to pursue a true LT via the draft. Has 38 career starts across four O-line positions. Allowed 15 pressures and 2 sacks for Miami in 2025; PFF graded him 35th among tackles in the second half of 2025. One-year deal signals bridge solution — OT remains a tier-1 draft need. Partially addresses depth but does NOT solve the LT starter problem long-term.

contract.notes: 1-year, fully guaranteed per NFL Network/Ari Meirov.

signings.analysis: Replaces David Montgomery as Jahmyr Gibbs' complement, but at a fraction of the cost and with far less certainty. Pacheco had injury issues in 2025 (563 yards from scrimmage in 13 games) and has seen production decline since 2023. At $1.81M fully guaranteed, this is a prove-it deal — he is NOT a like-for-like replacement for Montgomery's power/goal-line role. RB depth remains a legitimate need, though not urgent given Gibbs' elite status. Team still likely wants to add depth via late-round draft pick.

contract.notes: Terms not yet publicly reported as of 2026-03-13. Expected to be a low-cost backup deal (veteran minimum to low single-digit millions).

signings.analysis: Familiar face returning as Jared Goff's backup after Kyle Allen departed to Buffalo. Bridgewater previously served as Goff's backup in 2023 and 2024. Known locker-room leader and mentor (especially to Jameson Williams). Has not started an NFL game since 2022. QB2 need is resolved for 2026 at a minimal cap cost. Lions may still target a developmental QB in the mid-rounds of the draft.

contract.notes: Guaranteed amount not confirmed. Re-signed with Lions.

signings.analysis: One of the more effective Lions FA signings from 2025; re-ups on another 1-year deal. Ya-Sin can play outside and slot, valuable with Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) recovering. Finished 2025 with 47 tackles, 9 PBUs, 51% completion rate allowed and 74 passer rating allowed. At $4M/year on a 1-year deal, this is depth-tier money — Ya-Sin is not a CB1 solution. CB remains a legitimate need for the draft, especially after Amik Robertson departed.

contract.notes: 1-year deal; exact terms not publicly reported as of 2026-03-13.

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 1-year deal to add depth after Alex Anzalone departed. Rodriguez was coming off a torn ACL in 2024 and played only 7 games in 2025 (12 tackles, 1 TFL). A homegrown 6th-round pick (2022) who is still proving himself healthy. His re-signing partially addresses LB depth behind Jack Campbell and Derrick Barnes, but the loss of Anzalone (defensive captain) and Grant Stuard (375 special-teams snaps) still leaves a meaningful LB gap. LB remains a mid-round draft priority.

contract.notes: 1-year deal; exact terms not publicly reported as of 2026-03-13.

signings.analysis: Nickel/safety hybrid signing from Tampa Bay. Recorded 165 total tackles over 3 seasons in Tampa; in 2025 logged 25 tackles, a forced fumble and a TFL in 14 games. Adds valuable versatility to a secondary decimated by injury (Brian Branch — Achilles, Kerby Joseph — knee). One-year deal is a depth move only. Safety remains a pressing need with both starters coming off season-ending injuries; Izien is insurance, not a replacement for an elite safety.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Taylor Decker's release request created a new, critical LT need. Borom is a 1-year bridge on a depth-tier deal — not a starter solution. Penei Sewell could shift to LT, but that creates a RT vacancy. The OT class in 2026 is deep (potentially 7 first-rounders), and Detroit sitting at pick #17 makes this the premier draft target. Escalate to tier 1 — this is the top need entering the draft.

Detroit lost ALL of its EDGE depth behind Aidan Hutchinson: Al-Quadin Muhammad (11 sacks, to Buccaneers), Tyrus Wheat (to Cowboys), Josh Paschal (released, IR all year). No meaningful EDGE was added in free agency. Hutchinson is elite but cannot carry the entire pass rush alone. EDGE is now a co-top-1 need with OT. Draft must address this with a high pick or multiple selections.

Cade Mays signing (3yr/$25M, $14M guaranteed) fills the center vacancy created by Glasgow's release. He's a legitimate starter who allowed 0 sacks in 726 snaps for Carolina. Juice Scruggs adds cheap depth. IOL drops from critical to depth need. Lions may still want a developmental guard given Tate Ratledge/Mahogany are young, but the acute center crisis is resolved.

Both Roy Lopez (2yr/$10.5M, Cardinals) and DJ Reader (UFA, unsigned) departed. Detroit's interior D-line is now dangerously thin — primarily younger/unproven players remain. No IDL was added in FA. Remains a tier 2 need and should be addressed in the mid-rounds of the draft. Lions are clearly looking to get younger and cheaper here, but the current depth is inadequate.

Amik Robertson departed (2yr/$16M, Commanders). Rock Ya-Sin re-signed on a 1-year depth deal ($4M). With Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) both recovering from season-ending injuries, CB/DB depth is critical. Ya-Sin minimally offsets the Robertson departure but doesn't solve CB2 long-term. CB remains a tier 2 need — priority in rounds 2-3 or via late FA.

Alex Anzalone (2yr/$17M, Buccaneers) and Grant Stuard (2yr/$5M, Rams) both departed. Malcolm Rodriguez re-signed on a 1-year prove-it deal coming off a torn ACL. Jack Campbell (All-Pro, 178 tackles) and Derrick Barnes anchor the room, but depth is thin. Rodriguez is not a Anzalone-level replacement as signal-caller. LB stays at tier 2 — need a veteran or mid-round draft pick to add depth and ST value.

David Montgomery traded to Texans. Isiah Pacheco signed on a 1-year, $1.81M fully guaranteed deal — a prove-it depth contract, not a starter investment. Jahmyr Gibbs remains an elite RB1 who can carry the load. The urgency of RB drops because Gibbs is elite, but the Lions still lack a reliable thunder to Gibbs' lightning. A late-round (4th-5th) power back in the draft would be ideal.

Brian Branch (Achilles) and Kerby Joseph (knee) are both recovering from season-ending injuries. Christian Izien (1-year, terms TBD) is depth/insurance only. Neither starter has been replaced or reinforced with a meaningful commitment. Safety remains a real concern — a developmental or veteran S should be targeted in rounds 3-5, and recovery timelines for Branch/Joseph will dictate how urgent this becomes.

Kyle Allen departed (2yr/$6.1M, Bills) but Teddy Bridgewater returns on a low-cost deal as Goff's proven backup. QB2 is resolved for 2026 at minimal cap cost. Lions may still draft a developmental QB in rounds 3-5 given Bridgewater's age (34 in November), but it's no longer an urgent need.

key_additions.headline: 26-year-old center fills Frank Ragnow's void with 0 sacks allowed in 726 snaps for Carolina in 2025
Detroit native bridge-starts at tackle after Decker's release; Sewell shift to LT in play
Two-time Super Bowl champion replaces Montgomery as Gibbs' complement on a minimum-tier deal

key_additions.contract_summary: Existing contract ($0 guaranteed remaining); acquired via David Montgomery trade
key_additions.headline: Former 2nd-round pick adds IOL depth as part of the Montgomery trade haul along with a 2026 4th-round pick
Versatile CB/S hybrid retained after Robertson departure; crucial depth with Branch and Joseph recovering
Beloved locker-room presence returns as Goff's backup for the third time in his Lions tenure
Fan-favorite 'Rodrigo' returns on prove-it deal to rebuild after torn ACL; provides LB depth behind Campbell
Versatile nickel/safety hybrid adds secondary depth while Branch and Joseph rehab season-ending injuries

key_losses.headline: Shocking departure of 10-year LT franchise cornerstone creates Detroit's most pressing draft need
Career-best 11-sack season in 2025 couldn't secure Detroit's bag; Lions now stripped of all EDGE depth behind Hutchinson
Multi-year defensive captain departs, removing the signal-caller from Detroit's linebacker room
Reliable CB who became a near-starter due to Lions' injury woes earned a legitimate payday elsewhere

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to Texans for Juice Scruggs + 2026 4th + 2027 7th
key_losses.headline: 'Sonic and Knuckles' era ends; Lions received solid draft capital and depth in return for the aging bruiser
Two-sack interior disruptor returns to Arizona, thinning Detroit's already-depleted D-line rotation
Overmatched center-of-necessity released after rough 2025 season filling in for retired Frank Ragnow
Five-year Lion and returner specialist reunites with former OC Ben Johnson in Chicago

confidence_notes: All signings and departures confirmed by multiple credible sources (NFL Network, ESPN, The Athletic, Detroit News, Detroit Free Press). Contract figures for Mays ($25M/3yr, $14M gtd), Ya-Sin ($4M/1yr), Pacheco ($1.81M/1yr fully guaranteed), Borom (1yr ~$2.5M per SI), Anzalone ($17M/2yr), Robertson ($16M/2yr), Lopez ($10.5-11.5M/2yr), Muhammad (up to $6M/1yr), and Stuard (up to $5.05M/2yr) are sourced from NFL Network/Spotrac/team reporters. Bridgewater, Rodriguez, and Izien contract dollar amounts not yet publicly confirmed as of 2026-03-13. Larry Borom's exact guaranteed money not yet reported; total value estimated from SI reporting (~$2.5M). Cap figures sourced from OverTheCap.
